chief_complaint: A 14-year-old female presented with a 12-day history of right-sided weakness and speech difficulties.
history: Cerebral infarction secondary to ECMO is a commonly reported complication in the pediatric population. The patient developed a right middle cerebral artery (MCA) acute ischemic stroke (AIS) 12 days after veno-arterial ECMO (VA-ECMO) decannulation.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 20/min. Physical examination revealed right-sided weakness and dysarthria.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: normal. Imaging: MRI showing a right MCA infarct. Other tests: none.
correct_diagnosis: Right middle cerebral artery (MCA) acute ischemic stroke (AIS) secondary to ECMO
differentials: Right middle cerebral artery (MCA) acute ischemic stroke (AIS) secondary to ECMO
correct_ranking: Right middle cerebral artery (MCA) acute ischemic stroke (AIS) secondary to ECMO
